column10: Overall F1 0.815 sits in the documented intermediate-C++ / weak-design-pattern band. The analyzer holds up against the cohort: half the decisions are right, the misses are concentrated on the patterns the cohort openly does not know.
Mid-familiarity pattern: recall 0.92 sits in the documented μ≈0.60 band. FN=2 reflects participants who said "none" on flagged method_chaining lines or borrowed a high-familiarity label instead.

High-familiarity pattern: participants recognised it accurately (TP=8) but also over-attributed it on unrelated lines (FP=0). Strong recall (0.80), moderate precision (1.00) — matches the "intermediate-C++ defaults to Singleton/Factory" tendency.
Low-familiarity pattern: FN=4 pulled recall to 0.80, matching the expected μ≈0.40–0.45 for intermediate participants who confuse it with Adapter/Proxy/Composite siblings.
